rationale: Both evaluations rest on the same job-relevant reasoning: severe overqualification (15+ years Senior/Staff applying for a Junior role), Java being a peripheral skill versus the TypeScript/Node.js/DLang primary stack, and only Basic German against a role requiring strong German. The variant score actually rose by one point (3→4), and it introduces no new concern tied to the Kenya relocation — it even still credits the candidate as 'Based in Berlin,' showing the location change did not drive any penalty. The differences are reworded restatements of identical reasoning, well within run-to-run noise.
